title: Fluffy Indian Butternut Squash Stir-fry
description: A light and herbal dish infused with spices from Kerala cuisine, this diabetic-friendly stir fry features tender butternut squash cooked to perfection.
instructions: Preheat your oven to 400°F (205°C). Cut the butternut squash into small cubes.
In a bowl, mix the chopped onion and garlic with a bit of salt. Cook until translucent, about 3-5 minutes on medium heat.
Add tomato puree to the onions and cook for another minute, stirring frequently to avoid burning.
Transfer the cooked onion mixture to a baking dish, then add butternut squash cubes. Drizzle with olive oil lightly, season with turmeric powder and garam masala.
Bake in preheated oven for about 15-20 minutes or until the vegetables are tender.
Stir in chopped coriander leaves towards the end of cooking time to preserve their bright green color. Adjust seasoning if needed.
Serve hot as a side dish with rotis, chapati or brown rice.
